anchor_evidence.snippet: Permeability of 8 mM aqueous solution of P+-Ibuprofen ion-pairs through membranes

The trans-isomer had a lag time of about 18 hours and reached equilibrium after 72 hours, whereas the cis-isomer readily penetrated through the model membrane and reached equilibrium in less than 12 hours following exponential patterns.
Both Ibuprofen ion-pairs formed an ionic salt, with the triethylammonium Ibuprofenate salt (N+-Ibuprofen) being liquid at room temperature...

notes: The paper discusses the photo-responsive properties of Ibuprofen when ion-paired with azobenzene, including permeability studies through synthetic skin membranes.
